name: Freeport-McMoRan Miami Smelter
capacity_notes: Smelter ~590kt concentrate; IsaSmelt + 4 Hoboken converters; rod mill on-site
notes: Only operating primary copper smelter in US besides Kennecott. IsaSmelt furnace (primary), ELKEM electric furnace (secondary), 4 Hoboken converters, acid plant. Processes concentrate from Morenci, Bagdad, Safford/Lone Star, Sierrita. SX/EW capacity 200M lbs/yr. Rod mill commissioned 1966.

name: Kennecott Utah Copper Smelter
capacity_notes: ~300kt anode; Outotec flash smelter + Mitsubishi converter
notes: Flash smelting furnace + Mitsubishi continuous converting. Major rebuild 2023 (offline May-Sep). Returned to normal operations Q1 2024. Processes concentrate from Bingham Canyon (world's deepest open pit). Acid plant, molybdenum plant co-located.

name: Kennecott Utah Copper Refinery
products: LME Grade A copper cathode, gold, silver, selenium, tellurium
markets: wire rod mills, brass mills, tube mills
notes: Electrolytic refinery co-located with smelter. Rebuilds completed 2023-2024. Produces precious metals (Au, Ag) and specialty metals (Se, Te) as byproducts. Cathode shipped to domestic rod/brass mills.

name: Freeport-McMoRan El Paso Refinery & Rod Mill
capacity_notes: Refinery capacity ~408kt cathode/yr (900M lbs); rod mill co-located
products: copper cathode, copper rod, nickel carbonate, copper telluride, precious metals slimes (Au, Ag, Pt, Pd)
notes: Sufficient capacity to refine all anode from Miami operation. Electrolytic refinery + continuous cast rod mill. One of world's largest copper rod producers. Copper Mark designation received 2021.

name: ASARCO Amarillo Refinery
notes: Idled October 2019 along with ASARCO Hayden smelter. Grupo Mexico ownership. No announced restart date.

name: Freeport-McMoRan Morenci SX/EW
capacity_notes: SX/EW ~800M lbs/yr cathode from leach solutions
notes: World's largest SX/EW copper operation. Part of Morenci mine complex (72% FCX, 15% Sumitomo, 13% SMM). SX/EW plant commissioned 1987.

name: Freeport-McMoRan Bagdad SX/EW
notes: One of the longest continuously operating SX/EW plants in the world (since 1970). Bagdad 2X expansion under study to double mine production.

name: Freeport-McMoRan Safford/Lone Star SX/EW
notes: Includes Lone Star mine (began 2020). Expansion projects underway to increase to 300M lbs/yr.

name: Freeport-McMoRan Sierrita SX/EW
notes: Open-pit copper-moly mine south of Tucson.

name: Freeport-McMoRan Tyrone SX/EW
notes: Open-pit porphyry copper mine. SX/EW only — no concentrator or smelter.

name: Freeport-McMoRan Chino/Santa Rita SX/EW
notes: Historic Chino mine (Santa Rita). Open-pit. Concentrator + SX/EW.

name: ASARCO Ray SX/EW
notes: Large open-pit copper mine with SX/EW + concentrator. Concentrate shipped to third-party smelters.

name: ASARCO Mission SX/EW
notes: Open-pit porphyry copper mine south of Tucson. Concentrator + SX/EW.

name: ASARCO Silver Bell SX/EW
notes: Oxide copper leaching operation NW of Tucson.

name: Capstone Pinto Valley SX/EW
notes: Open-pit copper mine. Concentrator + small SX/EW. Formerly BHP.

name: Excelsior Mining Gunnison SX/EW
notes: In-situ copper recovery (ISR). Innovative acid injection into oxide copper zone. Ramp-up delayed. Permitted capacity 125M lbs/yr.

name: Highland Copper Copperwood
notes: Michigan's Upper Peninsula copper district. Underground mine + flotation. Permitting stage.

name: Aurubis Richmond (Augusta)
products: blister copper (shipped to Europe for refining), precious metals
feedstock: PCBs, copper cables, e-scrap, complex recycling materials
notes: First large secondary smelter for multimetal recycling in US. $800M total investment. Ribbon-cutting Sep 2024. 2 recycling modules. Processes circuit boards, cables, e-scrap. Blister copper refined at Aurubis European sites. Module 2 online 2026.

name: Southwire Secondary Smelter
capacity_notes: Fire refining of high-grade scrap to supplement cathode purchases
notes: Integrated with Southwire's rod mill. Produces fire-refined copper from scrap to supplement purchased cathode.

name: ASARCO Hayden Smelter
capacity_notes: Flash smelter ~360kt anode; idled since Oct 2019
products: copper anode (was shipped to Amarillo refinery)
notes: Flash smelting furnace. Idled October 2019 alongside Amarillo refinery. Grupo Mexico ownership. No announced restart. Was the second primary smelter in US before shutdown.

name: Southwire Carrollton Copper Rod Mill
capacity_notes: 380kt+/yr rod; world's largest SCR copper rod mill; new SCR-9000S online ~2023
products: ETP copper rod (8, 12.7, 15.6, 19mm dia), wire & cable
markets: building wire, utility, industrial, data centers, military
feedstock: copper cathode (LME Grade A) + up to 20% high-grade scrap
notes: LARGEST CATHODE CONSUMER IN US. Southwire is #1 US wire & cable producer. SCR-9000S (Primetals) replaced 40yr old system. 54 tph capacity. Cathode sourced domestically (Freeport, Kennecott) + imports (Chile, Peru). 60+ manufacturing facilities total. HQ and primary rod mill in Carrollton. 50% of world's copper rod passes through SCR systems.

name: Encore Wire / Prysmian McKinney Rod Mill
capacity_notes: Integrated rod mill on 460-acre campus; largest buyer of copper scrap in US (as single site)
products: copper rod, NM-B cable, THHN, armored cable, UF-B, MV cable
feedstock: copper cathode (mostly from AZ), copper scrap
notes: Acquired by Prysmian Jul 2024 for $290/share. 3.5M sq ft campus. $500M Prysmian expansion over 4yr including new 650k sq ft medium voltage cable plant. Vertically integrated: rod mill + wire drawing + cable making + distribution. Cathode primarily sourced domestically from Arizona.

name: AmRod Port Newark Rod Mill
markets: wire drawing, communications, magnet wire, building wire
notes: Leading independent copper rod producer. Port Newark location ideal for imported cathode. Rail, truck, and sea access. Hedging and risk management services. Datamyne top HS 7403 importer.

name: SDI LaFarga Copperworks
company: SDI LaFarga (Steel Dynamics / LaFarga Group JV)
capacity_notes: Shaft furnace 213kt/yr (470M lbs); refining furnace 113kt/yr (250M lbs) in conjunction
products: FRHC copper rod (Advantage™), ETP copper rod (Elemental™), wire, can welding wire
markets: building wire, magnet wire, telecom, automotive, aerospace, data
feedstock: 100% reclaimed copper scrap (FRHC line); cathode + bare bright (ETP line since 2020)
notes: JV of Steel Dynamics + LaFarga Group (Spain). Primarily scrap-based but added cathode-fed ETP rod (Elemental™) in Sep 2020 with new shaft furnace. Proprietary Q-Select sorting for scrap. 1B+ lbs copper scrap diverted from landfill since inception.

name: Cerro Wire Hartselle Rod Mill
products: copper rod, building wire, THHN, NM-B, UF-B
notes: Marmon Group (Berkshire Hathaway). Integrated rod mill + wire & cable manufacturing.

name: Rea Magnet Wire Fort Wayne Rod Mill
notes: Magnet wire producer. Essex Furukawa is world's largest MW producer. Rod mill feeds downstream magnet wire drawing.

name: Southwire Heflin Wire & Cable
notes: Major wire & cable manufacturing expansion announced. Rod supplied from Carrollton rod mill.

name: Wieland Rolled Products East Alton (fka Olin Brass)
capacity_notes: 1M+ lbs finished product per working day; 60 copper alloys
products: copper alloy strip, sheet, foil, welded tube
feedstock: copper cathode, copper scrap, zinc, tin, other alloying metals
notes: LARGEST BRASS MILL IN NORTH AMERICA. Formerly Olin Brass, acquired by Wieland Group (Germany) Jul 2019. Founded by Franklin Olin (Western Cartridge). Integrated casting (horizontal DC + vertical DC), hot/cold rolling, annealing, finishing. Produces 60 distinct copper alloys. Downstream ops: Wieland Metal Services (E. Longmeadow MA), Wieland Fabricated Products (stamping), Fineweld Tube Products.

name: Wieland Metal Services (Diversified Metals)
products: slit strip, specialty processed copper alloy
notes: Acquired by Wieland Nov 2018 (formerly Diversified Metals Inc.). Service center / re-roller. Value-added processing of East Alton mill products.

name: Aurubis Buffalo
products: copper rod, cake, billet, custom continuous cast shapes
notes: Aurubis North America operations (formerly Luvata). Continuous casting of copper rod, cake, and specialty shapes. Great Lakes water access.

name: Mueller Brass Port Huron
products: copper alloy rod, bar, forgings, extrusions
notes: Original Mueller Metals Company (1917). Brass rod and bar production. Rod mill modernized 1971. Now serves OEM/industrial segment.

name: Mueller Covington Fittings
products: copper fittings, wrot copper elbows, tees, adapters
notes: Major copper fittings plant. 1,500+ SKUs. Serves plumbing wholesale and building materials retail.

name: Cerro Copper Products (Marmon/BH)
notes: Cerro Copper Products / Cerro Flow Products. Scrap-based fire refinery producing feed for tube mill. Part of Marmon Group (Berkshire Hathaway). Mississippi River adjacent.

name: Chase Brass & Copper (Wieland Chase)
products: brass rod, bar, shapes (free-machining and specialty)
notes: Leading North American producer of brass rod. Wieland Group acquired Chase 2018. Continuous casting + extrusion + cold drawing. Significant automotive and ammunition markets.

name: Luvata (Mitsubishi Materials) Appleton
notes: Specialty copper products. Formerly Luvata, now Mitsubishi Materials. High-purity oxygen-free copper (OFC) products.

name: PMX Industries Cedar Rapids
products: copper and copper alloy strip, sheet, plate
notes: Korean-owned (Poongsan Corp). Integrated brass mill producing strip/sheet. Major US Mint supplier. Ammunition cartridge brass.

name: Mueller Tube Fulton
capacity_notes: Largest Mueller tube facility in NA
products: water tube (straight lengths, coils), ACR tube, line sets
notes: Mueller's largest NA copper tube facility. 6,300-ton extrusion press. Billet casting facility completed 1999. Multiple modernization phases. 'One of the most efficient tube mills in the United States.'

name: Mueller Tube Wynne
notes: Acquired via Halstead Industries 1998. $24M modernization 2000. Tube for plumbing and air conditioning.

name: Mueller Tube Clinton
notes: Acquired via Halstead Industries 1998. Plumbing and ACR tube production.

name: Cerro Flow Products Pine Hall
notes: Cerro/Marmon tube manufacturing. Part of Berkshire Hathaway industrial group.

name: Engelhart CTP US (ECTP)
markets: distribution to rod mills, brass mills
notes: Major Panjiva consignee for HS 7403. Commodity trading house. Warehouses cathode for distribution to end-consumers.

name: GT Commodities
notes: Panjiva top HS 7403 importer. Physical commodity trading.

name: Geo. Wm. Rueff Inc.
notes: Panjiva top HS 7403 importer. New Orleans-based metals broker/trader. Handles imported cathode through Port of New Orleans.
